data.body:
REALIGNMENT + PLAN-REVIEW (2026-06, local build)

Realigned this epic from the superseded three-pass framing (9da1, OPEN at write time) onto the SHIPPED four-pass review KERNEL (3316/vivid-gang-day, closed): b744 is now a kernel CONSUMER (supply diff-assembler + finder/verify/coach prompts + code move-catalog; call verify_findings/pass3_over_findings/coach). code_review.py is confirmed a throwaway single-pass demo to RETIRE, not a migration target.

DEPENDENCY ANALYSIS (answering "what must be satisfied"): the kernel (3316), runner+v3 engine (e6d9), contract reviewer prompts (da27), and grounding oracle+detector registry (8f6c) are ALL shipped/closed. The only LIVE external blocker is d251 (AWS Gerrit provisioning) — and it gates ONLY the Gerrit voter + dogfood ACs, not the capability. The 9da1 blocker link is stale (superseded by 3316; both closed).

SPLIT DECISION: NOT split. The capability (passes 1-4 + overlays + deterministic verdict, exposed via the existing MCP/CLI surface) is buildable and testable on real diffs WITHOUT Gerrit. Rather than split, ACs are PARTITIONED into (A) Capability — unblocked now — and (B) Dogfood/Gerrit — gated on d251. Splitting remains optional if capability is to close independently of infra.

OVERLAY / agent-triggered escalation: the approved base->overlay escalation (Round-A glob triggers UNION base.recommend_overlays, deterministic overlay_union, one-hop, capped N=3, enum-constrained) is now a first-class AC. DERISKED with experiments on the real v3 engine (offline, no tokens): the agent's recommendation drove a second batch's membership; bogus ids dropped by the enum; the cap bounded fan-out; one-hop held. Kernel proven gate-agnostic with a code move-catalog. Conclusion: NO core workflow-engine change needed — only two trivial scripted ops (overlay_union, merge_findings) + consumer-seam plug-ins.

PLAN-REVIEW RESULT: rebar review-plan (local build, --source local) -> verdict PASS (no Layer-1 floor failures, no blocking findings). model=opus-4-8, verifier=sonnet-4-6, 19 LLM calls. 10 advisory + 9 overflow advisories (all minor/none). Valid advisories REMEDIATED in this revision: pinned cap N=3; chose v1 secrets/security path (wrap gitleaks+semgrep behind the oracle envelope); named the observable code_review_verdict artifact + sidecar; clarified the re-export shim contract (public surface preserved, implementation replaced); added a fail-closed RED-fixture test AC; added an implementation-sequencing section (WS1-6) addressing the "too many layers, no sequencing" finding.

CHILDREN: none currently. WS1-6 in the description are PROPOSED child stories (not yet created); WS1-5 unblocked today, WS6 waits on d251.
